title: Lexer: chunked input, UTF-8 decoding, and token scanning
description:
Implement the lexer runtime that reads input and produces tokens using generated lex functions.

Deliverables:
- lexer.go: Lexer struct with Advance, Skip, MarkEnd, AcceptToken, Lookahead, EOF, position tracking (byte offset + row/column via Length).
- Chunked input management: maintain current chunk from Input.Read() callback, handle chunk boundary crossing, buffer management.
- UTF-8 decoding with ASCII fast path (byte < 0x80 → single comparison + assignment, skip utf8.DecodeRune).
- Input interface and []byte convenience adapter (StringInput).
- Included ranges support: detect range exits, jump to next range, is_at_included_range_start flag.
- Use concrete *Lexer type (not interface) for hot path performance.

Tests:
- Lex JSON tokens using the hand-compiled JSON grammar from task 1
- Verify byte positions and row/column positions
- Test chunk boundary crossing (split input at various points)
- Test included ranges (single range, multiple ranges, range boundaries)
- ASCII vs multi-byte UTF-8 character handling
- EOF handling

Reference: docs/design.md §2, §5, C source: lexer.c, lexer.h
